headline: Universal theme park gets £1.3bn taxpayer support - as name is revealed
summary: Europe's first Universal theme park is being built in the UK with £1.3bn in taxpayer support, it has been announced.  Universal, which is owned by Sky News' US parent company Comcast, wants to open the attraction in 2031.  It is to be called Universal United Kingdom Resort and include a 500-room hotel and retail and entertainment complex.
